2516 * Show a qualifier expression (which is a List with implicit AND semantics)
2517 */
2518static void
2519show_qual(List *qual, const char *qlabel,
2520		  PlanState *planstate, List *ancestors,
2521		  bool useprefix, ExplainState *es)
2522{
2523	Node	   *node;
2524
2525	/* No work if empty qual */
2526	if (qual == NIL)
2527		return;
2528
2529	/* Convert AND list to explicit AND */
2530	node = (Node *) make_ands_explicit(qual);
2531
2532	/* And show it */
2533	show_expression(node, qlabel, planstate, ancestors, useprefix, es);
2534}
2535
2536/*
2537 * Show a qualifier expression for a scan plan node
2538 */
2539static void
2540show_scan_qual(List *qual, const char *qlabel,
2541			   PlanState *planstate, List *ancestors,
2542			   ExplainState *es)
2543{
2544	bool		useprefix;
2545
2546	useprefix = (IsA(planstate->plan, SubqueryScan) || es->verbose);
2547	show_qual(qual, qlabel, planstate, ancestors, useprefix, es);
2548}
2549
2550/*
2551 * Show a qualifier expression for an upper-level plan node
2552 */
2553static void
2554show_upper_qual(List *qual, const char *qlabel,
2555				PlanState *planstate, List *ancestors,
2556				ExplainState *es)
2557{
2558	bool		useprefix;
2559
2560	useprefix = (es->rtable_size > 1 || es->verbose);
2561	show_qual(qual, qlabel, planstate, ancestors, useprefix, es);
2562}
2563
2564/*
2565 * Show the sort keys for a Sort node.
2566 */
2567static void
2568show_sort_keys(SortState *sortstate, List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2569{
2570	Sort	   *plan = (Sort *) sortstate->ss.ps.plan;
2571
2572	show_sort_group_keys((PlanState *) sortstate, "Sort Key",
2573						 plan->numCols, 0, plan->sortColIdx,
2574						 plan->sortOperators, plan->collations,
2575						 plan->nullsFirst,
2576						 ancestors, es);
2577}
2578
2579/*
2580 * Show the sort keys for an IncrementalSort node.
2581 */
2582static void
2583show_incremental_sort_keys(IncrementalSortState *incrsortstate,
2584						   List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2585{
2586	IncrementalSort *plan = (IncrementalSort *) incrsortstate->ss.ps.plan;
2587
2588	show_sort_group_keys((PlanState *) incrsortstate, "Sort Key",
2589						 plan->sort.numCols, plan->nPresortedCols,
2590						 plan->sort.sortColIdx,
2591						 plan->sort.sortOperators, plan->sort.collations,
2592						 plan->sort.nullsFirst,
2593						 ancestors, es);
2594}
2595
2596/*
2597 * Likewise, for a MergeAppend node.
2598 */
2599static void
2600show_merge_append_keys(MergeAppendState *mstate, List *ancestors,
2601					   ExplainState *es)
2602{
2603	MergeAppend *plan = (MergeAppend *) mstate->ps.plan;
2604
2605	show_sort_group_keys((PlanState *) mstate, "Sort Key",
2606						 plan->numCols, 0, plan->sortColIdx,
2607						 plan->sortOperators, plan->collations,
2608						 plan->nullsFirst,
2609						 ancestors, es);
2610}
2611
2612/*
2613 * Show the grouping keys for an Agg node.
2614 */
2615static void
2616show_agg_keys(AggState *astate, List *ancestors,
2617			  ExplainState *es)
2618{
2619	Agg		   *plan = (Agg *) astate->ss.ps.plan;
2620
2621	if (plan->numCols > 0 || plan->groupingSets)
2622	{
2623		/* The key columns refer to the tlist of the child plan */
2624		ancestors = lcons(plan, ancestors);
2625
2626		if (plan->groupingSets)
2627			show_grouping_sets(outerPlanState(astate), plan, ancestors, es);
2628		else
2629			show_sort_group_keys(outerPlanState(astate), "Group Key",
2630								 plan->numCols, 0, plan->grpColIdx,
2631								 NULL, NULL, NULL,
2632								 ancestors, es);
2633
2634		ancestors = list_delete_first(ancestors);
2635	}
2636}
2637
2638static void
2639show_grouping_sets(PlanState *planstate, Agg *agg,
2640				   List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2641{
2642	List	   *context;
2643	bool		useprefix;
2644	ListCell   *lc;
2645
2646	/* Set up deparsing context */
2647	context = set_deparse_context_plan(es->deparse_cxt,
2648									   planstate->plan,
2649									   ancestors);
2650	useprefix = (es->rtable_size > 1 || es->verbose);
2651
2652	ExplainOpenGroup("Grouping Sets", "Grouping Sets", false, es);
2653
2654	show_grouping_set_keys(planstate, agg, NULL,
2655						   context, useprefix, ancestors, es);
2656
2657	foreach(lc, agg->chain)
2658	{
2659		Agg		   *aggnode = lfirst(lc);
2660		Sort	   *sortnode = (Sort *) aggnode->plan.lefttree;
2661
2662		show_grouping_set_keys(planstate, aggnode, sortnode,
2663							   context, useprefix, ancestors, es);
2664	}
2665
2666	ExplainCloseGroup("Grouping Sets", "Grouping Sets", false, es);
2667}
2668
2669static void
2670show_grouping_set_keys(PlanState *planstate,
2671					   Agg *aggnode, Sort *sortnode,
2672					   List *context, bool useprefix,
2673					   List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2674{
2675	Plan	   *plan = planstate->plan;
2676	char	   *exprstr;
2677	ListCell   *lc;
2678	List	   *gsets = aggnode->groupingSets;
2679	AttrNumber *keycols = aggnode->grpColIdx;
2680	const char *keyname;
2681	const char *keysetname;
2682
2683	if (aggnode->aggstrategy == AGG_HASHED || aggnode->aggstrategy == AGG_MIXED)
2684	{
2685		keyname = "Hash Key";
2686		keysetname = "Hash Keys";
2687	}
2688	else
2689	{
2690		keyname = "Group Key";
2691		keysetname = "Group Keys";
2692	}
2693
2694	ExplainOpenGroup("Grouping Set", NULL, true, es);
2695
2696	if (sortnode)
2697	{
2698		show_sort_group_keys(planstate, "Sort Key",
2699							 sortnode->numCols, 0, sortnode->sortColIdx,
2700							 sortnode->sortOperators, sortnode->collations,
2701							 sortnode->nullsFirst,
2702							 ancestors, es);
2703		if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
2704			es->indent++;
2705	}
2706
2707	ExplainOpenGroup(keysetname, keysetname, false, es);
2708
2709	foreach(lc, gsets)
2710	{
2711		List	   *result = NIL;
2712		ListCell   *lc2;
2713
2714		foreach(lc2, (List *) lfirst(lc))
2715		{
2716			Index		i = lfirst_int(lc2);
2717			AttrNumber	keyresno = keycols[i];
2718			TargetEntry *target = get_tle_by_resno(plan->targetlist,
2719												   keyresno);
2720
2721			if (!target)
2722				elog(ERROR, "no tlist entry for key %d", keyresno);
2723			/* Deparse the expression, showing any top-level cast */
2724			exprstr = deparse_expression((Node *) target->expr, context,
2725										 useprefix, true);
2726
2727			result = lappend(result, exprstr);
2728		}
2729
2730		if (!result && es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
2731			ExplainPropertyText(keyname, "()", es);
2732		else
2733			ExplainPropertyListNested(keyname, result, es);
2734	}
2735
2736	ExplainCloseGroup(keysetname, keysetname, false, es);
2737
2738	if (sortnode && es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
2739		es->indent--;
2740
2741	ExplainCloseGroup("Grouping Set", NULL, true, es);
2742}
2743
2744/*
2745 * Show the grouping keys for a Group node.
2746 */
2747static void
2748show_group_keys(GroupState *gstate, List *ancestors,
2749				ExplainState *es)
2750{
2751	Group	   *plan = (Group *) gstate->ss.ps.plan;
2752
2753	/* The key columns refer to the tlist of the child plan */
2754	ancestors = lcons(plan, ancestors);
2755	show_sort_group_keys(outerPlanState(gstate), "Group Key",
2756						 plan->numCols, 0, plan->grpColIdx,
2757						 NULL, NULL, NULL,
2758						 ancestors, es);
2759	ancestors = list_delete_first(ancestors);
2760}
2761
2762/*
2763 * Common code to show sort/group keys, which are represented in plan nodes
2764 * as arrays of targetlist indexes.  If it's a sort key rather than a group
2765 * key, also pass sort operators/collations/nullsFirst arrays.
2766 */
2767static void
2768show_sort_group_keys(PlanState *planstate, const char *qlabel,
2769					 int nkeys, int nPresortedKeys, AttrNumber *keycols,
2770					 Oid *sortOperators, Oid *collations, bool *nullsFirst,
2771					 List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2772{
2773	Plan	   *plan = planstate->plan;
2774	List	   *context;
2775	List	   *result = NIL;
2776	List	   *resultPresorted = NIL;
2777	StringInfoData sortkeybuf;
2778	bool		useprefix;
2779	int			keyno;
2780
2781	if (nkeys <= 0)
2782		return;
2783
2784	initStringInfo(&sortkeybuf);
2785
2786	/* Set up deparsing context */
2787	context = set_deparse_context_plan(es->deparse_cxt,
2788									   plan,
2789									   ancestors);
2790	useprefix = (es->rtable_size > 1 || es->verbose);
2791
2792	for (keyno = 0; keyno < nkeys; keyno++)
2793	{
2794		/* find key expression in tlist */
2795		AttrNumber	keyresno = keycols[keyno];
2796		TargetEntry *target = get_tle_by_resno(plan->targetlist,
2797											   keyresno);
2798		char	   *exprstr;
2799
2800		if (!target)
2801			elog(ERROR, "no tlist entry for key %d", keyresno);
2802		/* Deparse the expression, showing any top-level cast */
2803		exprstr = deparse_expression((Node *) target->expr, context,
2804									 useprefix, true);
2805		resetStringInfo(&sortkeybuf);
2806		appendStringInfoString(&sortkeybuf, exprstr);
2807		/* Append sort order information, if relevant */
2808		if (sortOperators != NULL)
2809			show_sortorder_options(&sortkeybuf,
2810								   (Node *) target->expr,
2811								   sortOperators[keyno],
2812								   collations[keyno],
2813								   nullsFirst[keyno]);
2814		/* Emit one property-list item per sort key */
2815		result = lappend(result, pstrdup(sortkeybuf.data));
2816		if (keyno < nPresortedKeys)
2817			resultPresorted = lappend(resultPresorted, exprstr);
2818	}
2819
2820	ExplainPropertyList(qlabel, result, es);
2821	if (nPresortedKeys > 0)
2822		ExplainPropertyList("Presorted Key", resultPresorted, es);
2823}
2824
2825/*
2826 * Append nondefault characteristics of the sort ordering of a column to buf
2827 * (collation, direction, NULLS FIRST/LAST)
2828 */
2829static void
2830show_sortorder_options(StringInfo buf, Node *sortexpr,
2831					   Oid sortOperator, Oid collation, bool nullsFirst)
2832{
2833	Oid			sortcoltype = exprType(sortexpr);
2834	bool		reverse = false;
2835	TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
2836
2837	typentry = lookup_type_cache(sortcoltype,
2838								 TYPECACHE_LT_OPR | TYPECACHE_GT_OPR);
2839
2840	/*
2841	 * Print COLLATE if it's not default for the column's type.  There are
2842	 * some cases where this is redundant, eg if expression is a column whose
2843	 * declared collation is that collation, but it's hard to distinguish that
2844	 * here (and arguably, printing COLLATE explicitly is a good idea anyway
2845	 * in such cases).
2846	 */
2847	if (OidIsValid(collation) && collation != get_typcollation(sortcoltype))
2848	{
2849		char	   *collname = get_collation_name(collation);
2850
2851		if (collname == NULL)
2852			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for collation %u", collation);
2853		appendStringInfo(buf, " COLLATE %s", quote_identifier(collname));
2854	}
2855
2856	/* Print direction if not ASC, or USING if non-default sort operator */
2857	if (sortOperator == typentry->gt_opr)
2858	{
2859		appendStringInfoString(buf, " DESC");
2860		reverse = true;
2861	}
2862	else if (sortOperator != typentry->lt_opr)
2863	{
2864		char	   *opname = get_opname(sortOperator);
2865
2866		if (opname == NULL)
2867			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for operator %u", sortOperator);
2868		appendStringInfo(buf, " USING %s", opname);
2869		/* Determine whether operator would be considered ASC or DESC */
2870		(void) get_equality_op_for_ordering_op(sortOperator, &reverse);
2871	}
2872
2873	/* Add NULLS FIRST/LAST only if it wouldn't be default */
2874	if (nullsFirst && !reverse)
2875	{
2876		appendStringInfoString(buf, " NULLS FIRST");
2877	}
2878	else if (!nullsFirst && reverse)
2879	{
2880		appendStringInfoString(buf, " NULLS LAST");
2881	}
2882}
2883
2884/*
2885 * Show the window definition for a WindowAgg node.
2886 */
2887static void
2888show_window_def(WindowAggState *planstate, List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2889{
2890	WindowAgg  *wagg = (WindowAgg *) planstate->ss.ps.plan;
2891	StringInfoData wbuf;
2892	bool		needspace = false;
2893
2894	initStringInfo(&wbuf);
2895	appendStringInfo(&wbuf, "%s AS (", quote_identifier(wagg->winname));
2896
2897	/* The key columns refer to the tlist of the child plan */
2898	ancestors = lcons(wagg, ancestors);
2899	if (wagg->partNumCols > 0)
2900	{
2901		appendStringInfoString(&wbuf, "PARTITION BY ");
2902		show_window_keys(&wbuf, outerPlanState(planstate),
2903						 wagg->partNumCols, wagg->partColIdx,
2904						 ancestors, es);
2905		needspace = true;
2906	}
2907	if (wagg->ordNumCols > 0)
2908	{
2909		if (needspace)
2910			appendStringInfoChar(&wbuf, ' ');
2911		appendStringInfoString(&wbuf, "ORDER BY ");
2912		show_window_keys(&wbuf, outerPlanState(planstate),
2913						 wagg->ordNumCols, wagg->ordColIdx,
2914						 ancestors, es);
2915		needspace = true;
2916	}
2917	ancestors = list_delete_first(ancestors);
2918	if (wagg->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT)
2919	{
2920		List	   *context;
2921		bool		useprefix;
2922		char	   *framestr;
2923
2924		/* Set up deparsing context for possible frame expressions */
2925		context = set_deparse_context_plan(es->deparse_cxt,
2926										   (Plan *) wagg,
2927										   ancestors);
2928		useprefix = (es->rtable_size > 1 || es->verbose);
2929		framestr = get_window_frame_options_for_explain(wagg->frameOptions,
2930														wagg->startOffset,
2931														wagg->endOffset,
2932														context,
2933														useprefix);
2934		if (needspace)
2935			appendStringInfoChar(&wbuf, ' ');
2936		appendStringInfoString(&wbuf, framestr);
2937		pfree(framestr);
2938	}
2939	appendStringInfoChar(&wbuf, ')');
2940	ExplainPropertyText("Window", wbuf.data, es);
2941	pfree(wbuf.data);
2942}
2943
2944/*
2945 * Append the keys of a window's PARTITION BY or ORDER BY clause to buf.
2946 * We can't use show_sort_group_keys for this because that's too opinionated
2947 * about how the result will be displayed.
2948 * Note that the "planstate" node should be the WindowAgg's child.
2949 */
2950static void
2951show_window_keys(StringInfo buf, PlanState *planstate,
2952				 int nkeys, AttrNumber *keycols,
2953				 List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
2954{
2955	Plan	   *plan = planstate->plan;
2956	List	   *context;
2957	bool		useprefix;
2958
2959	/* Set up deparsing context */
2960	context = set_deparse_context_plan(es->deparse_cxt,
2961									   plan,
2962									   ancestors);
2963	useprefix = (es->rtable_size > 1 || es->verbose);
2964
2965	for (int keyno = 0; keyno < nkeys; keyno++)
2966	{
2967		/* find key expression in tlist */
2968		AttrNumber	keyresno = keycols[keyno];
2969		TargetEntry *target = get_tle_by_resno(plan->targetlist,
2970											   keyresno);
2971		char	   *exprstr;
2972
2973		if (!target)
2974			elog(ERROR, "no tlist entry for key %d", keyresno);
2975		/* Deparse the expression, showing any top-level cast */
2976		exprstr = deparse_expression((Node *) target->expr, context,
2977									 useprefix, true);
2978		if (keyno > 0)
2979			appendStringInfoString(buf, ", ");
2980		appendStringInfoString(buf, exprstr);
2981		pfree(exprstr);
2982
2983		/*
2984		 * We don't attempt to provide sort order information because
2985		 * WindowAgg carries equality operators not comparison operators;
2986		 * compare show_agg_keys.
2987		 */
2988	}
2989}
2990
2991/*
2992 * Show information on storage method and maximum memory/disk space used.
2993 */
2994static void
2995show_storage_info(char *maxStorageType, int64 maxSpaceUsed, ExplainState *es)
2996{
2997	int64		maxSpaceUsedKB = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(maxSpaceUsed);
2998
2999	if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3000	{
3001		ExplainPropertyText("Storage", maxStorageType, es);
3002		ExplainPropertyInteger("Maximum Storage", "kB", maxSpaceUsedKB, es);
3003	}
3004	else
3005	{
3006		ExplainIndentText(es);
3007		appendStringInfo(es->str,
3008						 "Storage: %s  Maximum Storage: " INT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3009						 maxStorageType,
3010						 maxSpaceUsedKB);
3011	}
3012}
3013
3014/*
3015 * Show TABLESAMPLE properties
3016 */
3017static void
3018show_tablesample(TableSampleClause *tsc, PlanState *planstate,
3019				 List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
3020{
3021	List	   *context;
3022	bool		useprefix;
3023	char	   *method_name;
3024	List	   *params = NIL;
3025	char	   *repeatable;
3026	ListCell   *lc;
3027
3028	/* Set up deparsing context */
3029	context = set_deparse_context_plan(es->deparse_cxt,
3030									   planstate->plan,
3031									   ancestors);
3032	useprefix = es->rtable_size > 1;
3033
3034	/* Get the tablesample method name */
3035	method_name = get_func_name(tsc->tsmhandler);
3036
3037	/* Deparse parameter expressions */
3038	foreach(lc, tsc->args)
3039	{
3040		Node	   *arg = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
3041
3042		params = lappend(params,
3043						 deparse_expression(arg, context,
3044											useprefix, false));
3045	}
3046	if (tsc->repeatable)
3047		repeatable = deparse_expression((Node *) tsc->repeatable, context,
3048										useprefix, false);
3049	else
3050		repeatable = NULL;
3051
3052	/* Print results */
3053	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3054	{
3055		bool		first = true;
3056
3057		ExplainIndentText(es);
3058		appendStringInfo(es->str, "Sampling: %s (", method_name);
3059		foreach(lc, params)
3060		{
3061			if (!first)
3062				appendStringInfoString(es->str, ", ");
3063			appendStringInfoString(es->str, (const char *) lfirst(lc));
3064			first = false;
3065		}
3066		appendStringInfoChar(es->str, ')');
3067		if (repeatable)
3068			appendStringInfo(es->str, " REPEATABLE (%s)", repeatable);
3069		appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3070	}
3071	else
3072	{
3073		ExplainPropertyText("Sampling Method", method_name, es);
3074		ExplainPropertyList("Sampling Parameters", params, es);
3075		if (repeatable)
3076			ExplainPropertyText("Repeatable Seed", repeatable, es);
3077	}
3078}
3079
3080/*
3081 * If it's EXPLAIN ANALYZE, show tuplesort stats for a sort node
3082 */
3083static void
3084show_sort_info(SortState *sortstate, ExplainState *es)
3085{
3086	if (!es->analyze)
3087		return;
3088
3089	if (sortstate->sort_Done && sortstate->tuplesortstate != NULL)
3090	{
3091		Tuplesortstate *state = (Tuplesortstate *) sortstate->tuplesortstate;
3092		TuplesortInstrumentation stats;
3093		const char *sortMethod;
3094		const char *spaceType;
3095		int64		spaceUsed;
3096
3097		tuplesort_get_stats(state, &stats);
3098		sortMethod = tuplesort_method_name(stats.sortMethod);
3099		spaceType = tuplesort_space_type_name(stats.spaceType);
3100		spaceUsed = stats.spaceUsed;
3101
3102		if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3103		{
3104			ExplainIndentText(es);
3105			appendStringInfo(es->str, "Sort Method: %s  %s: " INT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3106							 sortMethod, spaceType, spaceUsed);
3107		}
3108		else
3109		{
3110			ExplainPropertyText("Sort Method", sortMethod, es);
3111			ExplainPropertyInteger("Sort Space Used", "kB", spaceUsed, es);
3112			ExplainPropertyText("Sort Space Type", spaceType, es);
3113		}
3114	}
3115
3116	/*
3117	 * You might think we should just skip this stanza entirely when
3118	 * es->hide_workers is true, but then we'd get no sort-method output at
3119	 * all.  We have to make it look like worker 0's data is top-level data.
3120	 * This is easily done by just skipping the OpenWorker/CloseWorker calls.
3121	 * Currently, we don't worry about the possibility that there are multiple
3122	 * workers in such a case; if there are, duplicate output fields will be
3123	 * emitted.
3124	 */
3125	if (sortstate->shared_info != NULL)
3126	{
3127		int			n;
3128
3129		for (n = 0; n < sortstate->shared_info->num_workers; n++)
3130		{
3131			TuplesortInstrumentation *sinstrument;
3132			const char *sortMethod;
3133			const char *spaceType;
3134			int64		spaceUsed;
3135
3136			sinstrument = &sortstate->shared_info->sinstrument[n];
3137			if (sinstrument->sortMethod == SORT_TYPE_STILL_IN_PROGRESS)
3138				continue;		/* ignore any unfilled slots */
3139			sortMethod = tuplesort_method_name(sinstrument->sortMethod);
3140			spaceType = tuplesort_space_type_name(sinstrument->spaceType);
3141			spaceUsed = sinstrument->spaceUsed;
3142
3143			if (es->workers_state)
3144				ExplainOpenWorker(n, es);
3145
3146			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3147			{
3148				ExplainIndentText(es);
3149				appendStringInfo(es->str,
3150								 "Sort Method: %s  %s: " INT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3151								 sortMethod, spaceType, spaceUsed);
3152			}
3153			else
3154			{
3155				ExplainPropertyText("Sort Method", sortMethod, es);
3156				ExplainPropertyInteger("Sort Space Used", "kB", spaceUsed, es);
3157				ExplainPropertyText("Sort Space Type", spaceType, es);
3158			}
3159
3160			if (es->workers_state)
3161				ExplainCloseWorker(n, es);
3162		}
3163	}
3164}
3165
3166/*
3167 * Incremental sort nodes sort in (a potentially very large number of) batches,
3168 * so EXPLAIN ANALYZE needs to roll up the tuplesort stats from each batch into
3169 * an intelligible summary.
3170 *
3171 * This function is used for both a non-parallel node and each worker in a
3172 * parallel incremental sort node.
3173 */
3174static void
3175show_incremental_sort_group_info(IncrementalSortGroupInfo *groupInfo,
3176								 const char *groupLabel, bool indent, ExplainState *es)
3177{
3178	ListCell   *methodCell;
3179	List	   *methodNames = NIL;
3180
3181	/* Generate a list of sort methods used across all groups. */
3182	for (int bit = 0; bit < NUM_TUPLESORTMETHODS; bit++)
3183	{
3184		TuplesortMethod sortMethod = (1 << bit);
3185
3186		if (groupInfo->sortMethods & sortMethod)
3187		{
3188			const char *methodName = tuplesort_method_name(sortMethod);
3189
3190			methodNames = lappend(methodNames, unconstify(char *, methodName));
3191		}
3192	}
3193
3194	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3195	{
3196		if (indent)
3197			appendStringInfoSpaces(es->str, es->indent * 2);
3198		appendStringInfo(es->str, "%s Groups: " INT64_FORMAT "  Sort Method", groupLabel,
3199						 groupInfo->groupCount);
3200		/* plural/singular based on methodNames size */
3201		if (list_length(methodNames) > 1)
3202			appendStringInfoString(es->str, "s: ");
3203		else
3204			appendStringInfoString(es->str, ": ");
3205		foreach(methodCell, methodNames)
3206		{
3207			appendStringInfoString(es->str, (char *) methodCell->ptr_value);
3208			if (foreach_current_index(methodCell) < list_length(methodNames) - 1)
3209				appendStringInfoString(es->str, ", ");
3210		}
3211
3212		if (groupInfo->maxMemorySpaceUsed > 0)
3213		{
3214			int64		avgSpace = groupInfo->totalMemorySpaceUsed / groupInfo->groupCount;
3215			const char *spaceTypeName;
3216
3217			spaceTypeName = tuplesort_space_type_name(SORT_SPACE_TYPE_MEMORY);
3218			appendStringInfo(es->str, "  Average %s: " INT64_FORMAT "kB  Peak %s: " INT64_FORMAT "kB",
3219							 spaceTypeName, avgSpace,
3220							 spaceTypeName, groupInfo->maxMemorySpaceUsed);
3221		}
3222
3223		if (groupInfo->maxDiskSpaceUsed > 0)
3224		{
3225			int64		avgSpace = groupInfo->totalDiskSpaceUsed / groupInfo->groupCount;
3226
3227			const char *spaceTypeName;
3228
3229			spaceTypeName = tuplesort_space_type_name(SORT_SPACE_TYPE_DISK);
3230			appendStringInfo(es->str, "  Average %s: " INT64_FORMAT "kB  Peak %s: " INT64_FORMAT "kB",
3231							 spaceTypeName, avgSpace,
3232							 spaceTypeName, groupInfo->maxDiskSpaceUsed);
3233		}
3234	}
3235	else
3236	{
3237		StringInfoData groupName;
3238
3239		initStringInfo(&groupName);
3240		appendStringInfo(&groupName, "%s Groups", groupLabel);
3241		ExplainOpenGroup("Incremental Sort Groups", groupName.data, true, es);
3242		ExplainPropertyInteger("Group Count", NULL, groupInfo->groupCount, es);
3243
3244		ExplainPropertyList("Sort Methods Used", methodNames, es);
3245
3246		if (groupInfo->maxMemorySpaceUsed > 0)
3247		{
3248			int64		avgSpace = groupInfo->totalMemorySpaceUsed / groupInfo->groupCount;
3249			const char *spaceTypeName;
3250			StringInfoData memoryName;
3251
3252			spaceTypeName = tuplesort_space_type_name(SORT_SPACE_TYPE_MEMORY);
3253			initStringInfo(&memoryName);
3254			appendStringInfo(&memoryName, "Sort Space %s", spaceTypeName);
3255			ExplainOpenGroup("Sort Space", memoryName.data, true, es);
3256
3257			ExplainPropertyInteger("Average Sort Space Used", "kB", avgSpace, es);
3258			ExplainPropertyInteger("Peak Sort Space Used", "kB",
3259								   groupInfo->maxMemorySpaceUsed, es);
3260
3261			ExplainCloseGroup("Sort Space", memoryName.data, true, es);
3262		}
3263		if (groupInfo->maxDiskSpaceUsed > 0)
3264		{
3265			int64		avgSpace = groupInfo->totalDiskSpaceUsed / groupInfo->groupCount;
3266			const char *spaceTypeName;
3267			StringInfoData diskName;
3268
3269			spaceTypeName = tuplesort_space_type_name(SORT_SPACE_TYPE_DISK);
3270			initStringInfo(&diskName);
3271			appendStringInfo(&diskName, "Sort Space %s", spaceTypeName);
3272			ExplainOpenGroup("Sort Space", diskName.data, true, es);
3273
3274			ExplainPropertyInteger("Average Sort Space Used", "kB", avgSpace, es);
3275			ExplainPropertyInteger("Peak Sort Space Used", "kB",
3276								   groupInfo->maxDiskSpaceUsed, es);
3277
3278			ExplainCloseGroup("Sort Space", diskName.data, true, es);
3279		}
3280
3281		ExplainCloseGroup("Incremental Sort Groups", groupName.data, true, es);
3282	}
3283}
3284
3285/*
3286 * If it's EXPLAIN ANALYZE, show tuplesort stats for an incremental sort node
3287 */
3288static void
3289show_incremental_sort_info(IncrementalSortState *incrsortstate,
3290						   ExplainState *es)
3291{
3292	IncrementalSortGroupInfo *fullsortGroupInfo;
3293	IncrementalSortGroupInfo *prefixsortGroupInfo;
3294
3295	fullsortGroupInfo = &incrsortstate->incsort_info.fullsortGroupInfo;
3296
3297	if (!es->analyze)
3298		return;
3299
3300	/*
3301	 * Since we never have any prefix groups unless we've first sorted a full
3302	 * groups and transitioned modes (copying the tuples into a prefix group),
3303	 * we don't need to do anything if there were 0 full groups.
3304	 *
3305	 * We still have to continue after this block if there are no full groups,
3306	 * though, since it's possible that we have workers that did real work
3307	 * even if the leader didn't participate.
3308	 */
3309	if (fullsortGroupInfo->groupCount > 0)
3310	{
3311		show_incremental_sort_group_info(fullsortGroupInfo, "Full-sort", true, es);
3312		prefixsortGroupInfo = &incrsortstate->incsort_info.prefixsortGroupInfo;
3313		if (prefixsortGroupInfo->groupCount > 0)
3314		{
3315			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3316				appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3317			show_incremental_sort_group_info(prefixsortGroupInfo, "Pre-sorted", true, es);
3318		}
3319		if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3320			appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3321	}
3322
3323	if (incrsortstate->shared_info != NULL)
3324	{
3325		int			n;
3326		bool		indent_first_line;
3327
3328		for (n = 0; n < incrsortstate->shared_info->num_workers; n++)
3329		{
3330			IncrementalSortInfo *incsort_info =
3331				&incrsortstate->shared_info->sinfo[n];
3332
3333			/*
3334			 * If a worker hasn't processed any sort groups at all, then
3335			 * exclude it from output since it either didn't launch or didn't
3336			 * contribute anything meaningful.
3337			 */
3338			fullsortGroupInfo = &incsort_info->fullsortGroupInfo;
3339
3340			/*
3341			 * Since we never have any prefix groups unless we've first sorted
3342			 * a full groups and transitioned modes (copying the tuples into a
3343			 * prefix group), we don't need to do anything if there were 0
3344			 * full groups.
3345			 */
3346			if (fullsortGroupInfo->groupCount == 0)
3347				continue;
3348
3349			if (es->workers_state)
3350				ExplainOpenWorker(n, es);
3351
3352			indent_first_line = es->workers_state == NULL || es->verbose;
3353			show_incremental_sort_group_info(fullsortGroupInfo, "Full-sort",
3354											 indent_first_line, es);
3355			prefixsortGroupInfo = &incsort_info->prefixsortGroupInfo;
3356			if (prefixsortGroupInfo->groupCount > 0)
3357			{
3358				if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3359					appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3360				show_incremental_sort_group_info(prefixsortGroupInfo, "Pre-sorted", true, es);
3361			}
3362			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3363				appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3364
3365			if (es->workers_state)
3366				ExplainCloseWorker(n, es);
3367		}
3368	}
3369}
3370
3371/*
3372 * Show information on hash buckets/batches.
3373 */
3374static void
3375show_hash_info(HashState *hashstate, ExplainState *es)
3376{
3377	HashInstrumentation hinstrument = {0};
3378
3379	/*
3380	 * Collect stats from the local process, even when it's a parallel query.
3381	 * In a parallel query, the leader process may or may not have run the
3382	 * hash join, and even if it did it may not have built a hash table due to
3383	 * timing (if it started late it might have seen no tuples in the outer
3384	 * relation and skipped building the hash table).  Therefore we have to be
3385	 * prepared to get instrumentation data from all participants.
3386	 */
3387	if (hashstate->hinstrument)
3388		memcpy(&hinstrument, hashstate->hinstrument,
3389			   sizeof(HashInstrumentation));
3390
3391	/*
3392	 * Merge results from workers.  In the parallel-oblivious case, the
3393	 * results from all participants should be identical, except where
3394	 * participants didn't run the join at all so have no data.  In the
3395	 * parallel-aware case, we need to consider all the results.  Each worker
3396	 * may have seen a different subset of batches and we want to report the
3397	 * highest memory usage across all batches.  We take the maxima of other
3398	 * values too, for the same reasons as in ExecHashAccumInstrumentation.
3399	 */
3400	if (hashstate->shared_info)
3401	{
3402		SharedHashInfo *shared_info = hashstate->shared_info;
3403		int			i;
3404
3405		for (i = 0; i < shared_info->num_workers; ++i)
3406		{
3407			HashInstrumentation *worker_hi = &shared_info->hinstrument[i];
3408
3409			hinstrument.nbuckets = Max(hinstrument.nbuckets,
3410									   worker_hi->nbuckets);
3411			hinstrument.nbuckets_original = Max(hinstrument.nbuckets_original,
3412												worker_hi->nbuckets_original);
3413			hinstrument.nbatch = Max(hinstrument.nbatch,
3414									 worker_hi->nbatch);
3415			hinstrument.nbatch_original = Max(hinstrument.nbatch_original,
3416											  worker_hi->nbatch_original);
3417			hinstrument.space_peak = Max(hinstrument.space_peak,
3418										 worker_hi->space_peak);
3419		}
3420	}
3421
3422	if (hinstrument.nbatch > 0)
3423	{
3424		uint64		spacePeakKb = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(hinstrument.space_peak);
3425
3426		if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3427		{
3428			ExplainPropertyInteger("Hash Buckets", NULL,
3429								   hinstrument.nbuckets, es);
3430			ExplainPropertyInteger("Original Hash Buckets", NULL,
3431								   hinstrument.nbuckets_original, es);
3432			ExplainPropertyInteger("Hash Batches", NULL,
3433								   hinstrument.nbatch, es);
3434			ExplainPropertyInteger("Original Hash Batches", NULL,
3435								   hinstrument.nbatch_original, es);
3436			ExplainPropertyUInteger("Peak Memory Usage", "kB",
3437									spacePeakKb, es);
3438		}
3439		else if (hinstrument.nbatch_original != hinstrument.nbatch ||
3440				 hinstrument.nbuckets_original != hinstrument.nbuckets)
3441		{
3442			ExplainIndentText(es);
3443			appendStringInfo(es->str,
3444							 "Buckets: %d (originally %d)  Batches: %d (originally %d)  Memory Usage: " UINT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3445							 hinstrument.nbuckets,
3446							 hinstrument.nbuckets_original,
3447							 hinstrument.nbatch,
3448							 hinstrument.nbatch_original,
3449							 spacePeakKb);
3450		}
3451		else
3452		{
3453			ExplainIndentText(es);
3454			appendStringInfo(es->str,
3455							 "Buckets: %d  Batches: %d  Memory Usage: " UINT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3456							 hinstrument.nbuckets, hinstrument.nbatch,
3457							 spacePeakKb);
3458		}
3459	}
3460}
3461
3462/*
3463 * Show information on material node, storage method and maximum memory/disk
3464 * space used.
3465 */
3466static void
3467show_material_info(MaterialState *mstate, ExplainState *es)
3468{
3469	char	   *maxStorageType;
3470	int64		maxSpaceUsed;
3471
3472	Tuplestorestate *tupstore = mstate->tuplestorestate;
3473
3474	/*
3475	 * Nothing to show if ANALYZE option wasn't used or if execution didn't
3476	 * get as far as creating the tuplestore.
3477	 */
3478	if (!es->analyze || tupstore == NULL)
3479		return;
3480
3481	tuplestore_get_stats(tupstore, &maxStorageType, &maxSpaceUsed);
3482	show_storage_info(maxStorageType, maxSpaceUsed, es);
3483}
3484
3485/*
3486 * Show information on WindowAgg node, storage method and maximum memory/disk
3487 * space used.
3488 */
3489static void
3490show_windowagg_info(WindowAggState *winstate, ExplainState *es)
3491{
3492	char	   *maxStorageType;
3493	int64		maxSpaceUsed;
3494
3495	Tuplestorestate *tupstore = winstate->buffer;
3496
3497	/*
3498	 * Nothing to show if ANALYZE option wasn't used or if execution didn't
3499	 * get as far as creating the tuplestore.
3500	 */
3501	if (!es->analyze || tupstore == NULL)
3502		return;
3503
3504	tuplestore_get_stats(tupstore, &maxStorageType, &maxSpaceUsed);
3505	show_storage_info(maxStorageType, maxSpaceUsed, es);
3506}
3507
3508/*
3509 * Show information on CTE Scan node, storage method and maximum memory/disk
3510 * space used.
3511 */
3512static void
3513show_ctescan_info(CteScanState *ctescanstate, ExplainState *es)
3514{
3515	char	   *maxStorageType;
3516	int64		maxSpaceUsed;
3517
3518	Tuplestorestate *tupstore = ctescanstate->leader->cte_table;
3519
3520	if (!es->analyze || tupstore == NULL)
3521		return;
3522
3523	tuplestore_get_stats(tupstore, &maxStorageType, &maxSpaceUsed);
3524	show_storage_info(maxStorageType, maxSpaceUsed, es);
3525}
3526
3527/*
3528 * Show information on Table Function Scan node, storage method and maximum
3529 * memory/disk space used.
3530 */
3531static void
3532show_table_func_scan_info(TableFuncScanState *tscanstate, ExplainState *es)
3533{
3534	char	   *maxStorageType;
3535	int64		maxSpaceUsed;
3536
3537	Tuplestorestate *tupstore = tscanstate->tupstore;
3538
3539	if (!es->analyze || tupstore == NULL)
3540		return;
3541
3542	tuplestore_get_stats(tupstore, &maxStorageType, &maxSpaceUsed);
3543	show_storage_info(maxStorageType, maxSpaceUsed, es);
3544}
3545
3546/*
3547 * Show information on Recursive Union node, storage method and maximum
3548 * memory/disk space used.
3549 */
3550static void
3551show_recursive_union_info(RecursiveUnionState *rstate, ExplainState *es)
3552{
3553	char	   *maxStorageType,
3554			   *tempStorageType;
3555	int64		maxSpaceUsed,
3556				tempSpaceUsed;
3557
3558	if (!es->analyze)
3559		return;
3560
3561	/*
3562	 * Recursive union node uses two tuplestores.  We employ the storage type
3563	 * from one of them which consumed more memory/disk than the other.  The
3564	 * storage size is sum of the two.
3565	 */
3566	tuplestore_get_stats(rstate->working_table, &tempStorageType,
3567						 &tempSpaceUsed);
3568	tuplestore_get_stats(rstate->intermediate_table, &maxStorageType,
3569						 &maxSpaceUsed);
3570
3571	if (tempSpaceUsed > maxSpaceUsed)
3572		maxStorageType = tempStorageType;
3573
3574	maxSpaceUsed += tempSpaceUsed;
3575	show_storage_info(maxStorageType, maxSpaceUsed, es);
3576}
3577
3578/*
3579 * Show information on memoize hits/misses/evictions and memory usage.
3580 */
3581static void
3582show_memoize_info(MemoizeState *mstate, List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
3583{
3584	Plan	   *plan = ((PlanState *) mstate)->plan;
3585	ListCell   *lc;
3586	List	   *context;
3587	StringInfoData keystr;
3588	char	   *separator = "";
3589	bool		useprefix;
3590	int64		memPeakKb;
3591
3592	initStringInfo(&keystr);
3593
3594	/*
3595	 * It's hard to imagine having a memoize node with fewer than 2 RTEs, but
3596	 * let's just keep the same useprefix logic as elsewhere in this file.
3597	 */
3598	useprefix = es->rtable_size > 1 || es->verbose;
3599
3600	/* Set up deparsing context */
3601	context = set_deparse_context_plan(es->deparse_cxt,
3602									   plan,
3603									   ancestors);
3604
3605	foreach(lc, ((Memoize *) plan)->param_exprs)
3606	{
3607		Node	   *expr = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
3608
3609		appendStringInfoString(&keystr, separator);
3610
3611		appendStringInfoString(&keystr, deparse_expression(expr, context,
3612														   useprefix, false));
3613		separator = ", ";
3614	}
3615
3616	ExplainPropertyText("Cache Key", keystr.data, es);
3617	ExplainPropertyText("Cache Mode", mstate->binary_mode ? "binary" : "logical", es);
3618
3619	pfree(keystr.data);
3620
3621	if (!es->analyze)
3622		return;
3623
3624	if (mstate->stats.cache_misses > 0)
3625	{
3626		/*
3627		 * mem_peak is only set when we freed memory, so we must use mem_used
3628		 * when mem_peak is 0.
3629		 */
3630		if (mstate->stats.mem_peak > 0)
3631			memPeakKb = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(mstate->stats.mem_peak);
3632		else
3633			memPeakKb = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(mstate->mem_used);
3634
3635		if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3636		{
3637			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Hits", NULL, mstate->stats.cache_hits, es);
3638			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Misses", NULL, mstate->stats.cache_misses, es);
3639			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Evictions", NULL, mstate->stats.cache_evictions, es);
3640			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Overflows", NULL, mstate->stats.cache_overflows, es);
3641			ExplainPropertyInteger("Peak Memory Usage", "kB", memPeakKb, es);
3642		}
3643		else
3644		{
3645			ExplainIndentText(es);
3646			appendStringInfo(es->str,
3647							 "Hits: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Misses: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Evictions: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Overflows: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Memory Usage: " INT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3648							 mstate->stats.cache_hits,
3649							 mstate->stats.cache_misses,
3650							 mstate->stats.cache_evictions,
3651							 mstate->stats.cache_overflows,
3652							 memPeakKb);
3653		}
3654	}
3655
3656	if (mstate->shared_info == NULL)
3657		return;
3658
3659	/* Show details from parallel workers */
3660	for (int n = 0; n < mstate->shared_info->num_workers; n++)
3661	{
3662		MemoizeInstrumentation *si;
3663
3664		si = &mstate->shared_info->sinstrument[n];
3665
3666		/*
3667		 * Skip workers that didn't do any work.  We needn't bother checking
3668		 * for cache hits as a miss will always occur before a cache hit.
3669		 */
3670		if (si->cache_misses == 0)
3671			continue;
3672
3673		if (es->workers_state)
3674			ExplainOpenWorker(n, es);
3675
3676		/*
3677		 * Since the worker's MemoizeState.mem_used field is unavailable to
3678		 * us, ExecEndMemoize will have set the
3679		 * MemoizeInstrumentation.mem_peak field for us.  No need to do the
3680		 * zero checks like we did for the serial case above.
3681		 */
3682		memPeakKb = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(si->mem_peak);
3683
3684		if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3685		{
3686			ExplainIndentText(es);
3687			appendStringInfo(es->str,
3688							 "Hits: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Misses: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Evictions: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Overflows: " UINT64_FORMAT "  Memory Usage: " INT64_FORMAT "kB\n",
3689							 si->cache_hits, si->cache_misses,
3690							 si->cache_evictions, si->cache_overflows,
3691							 memPeakKb);
3692		}
3693		else
3694		{
3695			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Hits", NULL,
3696								   si->cache_hits, es);
3697			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Misses", NULL,
3698								   si->cache_misses, es);
3699			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Evictions", NULL,
3700								   si->cache_evictions, es);
3701			ExplainPropertyInteger("Cache Overflows", NULL,
3702								   si->cache_overflows, es);
3703			ExplainPropertyInteger("Peak Memory Usage", "kB", memPeakKb,
3704								   es);
3705		}
3706
3707		if (es->workers_state)
3708			ExplainCloseWorker(n, es);
3709	}
3710}
3711
3712/*
3713 * Show information on hash aggregate memory usage and batches.
3714 */
3715static void
3716show_hashagg_info(AggState *aggstate, ExplainState *es)
3717{
3718	Agg		   *agg = (Agg *) aggstate->ss.ps.plan;
3719	int64		memPeakKb = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(aggstate->hash_mem_peak);
3720
3721	if (agg->aggstrategy != AGG_HASHED &&
3722		agg->aggstrategy != AGG_MIXED)
3723		return;
3724
3725	if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3726	{
3727		if (es->costs)
3728			ExplainPropertyInteger("Planned Partitions", NULL,
3729								   aggstate->hash_planned_partitions, es);
3730
3731		/*
3732		 * During parallel query the leader may have not helped out.  We
3733		 * detect this by checking how much memory it used.  If we find it
3734		 * didn't do any work then we don't show its properties.
3735		 */
3736		if (es->analyze && aggstate->hash_mem_peak > 0)
3737		{
3738			ExplainPropertyInteger("HashAgg Batches", NULL,
3739								   aggstate->hash_batches_used, es);
3740			ExplainPropertyInteger("Peak Memory Usage", "kB", memPeakKb, es);
3741			ExplainPropertyInteger("Disk Usage", "kB",
3742								   aggstate->hash_disk_used, es);
3743		}
3744	}
3745	else
3746	{
3747		bool		gotone = false;
3748
3749		if (es->costs && aggstate->hash_planned_partitions > 0)
3750		{
3751			ExplainIndentText(es);
3752			appendStringInfo(es->str, "Planned Partitions: %d",
3753							 aggstate->hash_planned_partitions);
3754			gotone = true;
3755		}
3756
3757		/*
3758		 * During parallel query the leader may have not helped out.  We
3759		 * detect this by checking how much memory it used.  If we find it
3760		 * didn't do any work then we don't show its properties.
3761		 */
3762		if (es->analyze && aggstate->hash_mem_peak > 0)
3763		{
3764			if (!gotone)
3765				ExplainIndentText(es);
3766			else
3767				appendStringInfoSpaces(es->str, 2);
3768
3769			appendStringInfo(es->str, "Batches: %d  Memory Usage: " INT64_FORMAT "kB",
3770							 aggstate->hash_batches_used, memPeakKb);
3771			gotone = true;
3772
3773			/* Only display disk usage if we spilled to disk */
3774			if (aggstate->hash_batches_used > 1)
3775			{
3776				appendStringInfo(es->str, "  Disk Usage: " UINT64_FORMAT "kB",
3777								 aggstate->hash_disk_used);
3778			}
3779		}
3780
3781		if (gotone)
3782			appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3783	}
3784
3785	/* Display stats for each parallel worker */
3786	if (es->analyze && aggstate->shared_info != NULL)
3787	{
3788		for (int n = 0; n < aggstate->shared_info->num_workers; n++)
3789		{
3790			AggregateInstrumentation *sinstrument;
3791			uint64		hash_disk_used;
3792			int			hash_batches_used;
3793
3794			sinstrument = &aggstate->shared_info->sinstrument[n];
3795			/* Skip workers that didn't do anything */
3796			if (sinstrument->hash_mem_peak == 0)
3797				continue;
3798			hash_disk_used = sinstrument->hash_disk_used;
3799			hash_batches_used = sinstrument->hash_batches_used;
3800			memPeakKb = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(sinstrument->hash_mem_peak);
3801
3802			if (es->workers_state)
3803				ExplainOpenWorker(n, es);
3804
3805			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3806			{
3807				ExplainIndentText(es);
3808
3809				appendStringInfo(es->str, "Batches: %d  Memory Usage: " INT64_FORMAT "kB",
3810								 hash_batches_used, memPeakKb);
3811
3812				/* Only display disk usage if we spilled to disk */
3813				if (hash_batches_used > 1)
3814					appendStringInfo(es->str, "  Disk Usage: " UINT64_FORMAT "kB",
3815									 hash_disk_used);
3816				appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3817			}
3818			else
3819			{
3820				ExplainPropertyInteger("HashAgg Batches", NULL,
3821									   hash_batches_used, es);
3822				ExplainPropertyInteger("Peak Memory Usage", "kB", memPeakKb,
3823									   es);
3824				ExplainPropertyInteger("Disk Usage", "kB", hash_disk_used, es);
3825			}
3826
3827			if (es->workers_state)
3828				ExplainCloseWorker(n, es);
3829		}
3830	}
3831}
3832
3833/*
3834 * Show the total number of index searches for a
3835 * IndexScan/IndexOnlyScan/BitmapIndexScan node
3836 */
3837static void
3838show_indexsearches_info(PlanState *planstate, ExplainState *es)
3839{
3840	Plan	   *plan = planstate->plan;
3841	SharedIndexScanInstrumentation *SharedInfo = NULL;
3842	uint64		nsearches = 0;
3843
3844	if (!es->analyze)
3845		return;
3846
3847	/* Initialize counters with stats from the local process first */
3848	switch (nodeTag(plan))
3849	{
3850		case T_IndexScan:
3851			{
3852				IndexScanState *indexstate = ((IndexScanState *) planstate);
3853
3854				nsearches = indexstate->iss_Instrument.nsearches;
3855				SharedInfo = indexstate->iss_SharedInfo;
3856				break;
3857			}
3858		case T_IndexOnlyScan:
3859			{
3860				IndexOnlyScanState *indexstate = ((IndexOnlyScanState *) planstate);
3861
3862				nsearches = indexstate->ioss_Instrument.nsearches;
3863				SharedInfo = indexstate->ioss_SharedInfo;
3864				break;
3865			}
3866		case T_BitmapIndexScan:
3867			{
3868				BitmapIndexScanState *indexstate = ((BitmapIndexScanState *) planstate);
3869
3870				nsearches = indexstate->biss_Instrument.nsearches;
3871				SharedInfo = indexstate->biss_SharedInfo;
3872				break;
3873			}
3874		default:
3875			break;
3876	}
3877
3878	/* Next get the sum of the counters set within each and every process */
3879	if (SharedInfo)
3880	{
3881		for (int i = 0; i < SharedInfo->num_workers; ++i)
3882		{
3883			IndexScanInstrumentation *winstrument = &SharedInfo->winstrument[i];
3884
3885			nsearches += winstrument->nsearches;
3886		}
3887	}
3888
3889	ExplainPropertyUInteger("Index Searches", NULL, nsearches, es);
3890}
3891
3892/*
3893 * Show exact/lossy pages for a BitmapHeapScan node
3894 */
3895static void
3896show_tidbitmap_info(BitmapHeapScanState *planstate, ExplainState *es)
3897{
3898	if (!es->analyze)
3899		return;
3900
3901	if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3902	{
3903		ExplainPropertyUInteger("Exact Heap Blocks", NULL,
3904								planstate->stats.exact_pages, es);
3905		ExplainPropertyUInteger("Lossy Heap Blocks", NULL,
3906								planstate->stats.lossy_pages, es);
3907	}
3908	else
3909	{
3910		if (planstate->stats.exact_pages > 0 || planstate->stats.lossy_pages > 0)
3911		{
3912			ExplainIndentText(es);
3913			appendStringInfoString(es->str, "Heap Blocks:");
3914			if (planstate->stats.exact_pages > 0)
3915				appendStringInfo(es->str, " exact=" UINT64_FORMAT, planstate->stats.exact_pages);
3916			if (planstate->stats.lossy_pages > 0)
3917				appendStringInfo(es->str, " lossy=" UINT64_FORMAT, planstate->stats.lossy_pages);
3918			appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3919		}
3920	}
3921
3922	/* Display stats for each parallel worker */
3923	if (planstate->pstate != NULL)
3924	{
3925		for (int n = 0; n < planstate->sinstrument->num_workers; n++)
3926		{
3927			BitmapHeapScanInstrumentation *si = &planstate->sinstrument->sinstrument[n];
3928
3929			if (si->exact_pages == 0 && si->lossy_pages == 0)
3930				continue;
3931
3932			if (es->workers_state)
3933				ExplainOpenWorker(n, es);
3934
3935			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3936			{
3937				ExplainIndentText(es);
3938				appendStringInfoString(es->str, "Heap Blocks:");
3939				if (si->exact_pages > 0)
3940					appendStringInfo(es->str, " exact=" UINT64_FORMAT, si->exact_pages);
3941				if (si->lossy_pages > 0)
3942					appendStringInfo(es->str, " lossy=" UINT64_FORMAT, si->lossy_pages);
3943				appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
3944			}
3945			else
3946			{
3947				ExplainPropertyUInteger("Exact Heap Blocks", NULL,
3948										si->exact_pages, es);
3949				ExplainPropertyUInteger("Lossy Heap Blocks", NULL,
3950										si->lossy_pages, es);
3951			}
3952
3953			if (es->workers_state)
3954				ExplainCloseWorker(n, es);
3955		}
3956	}
3957}
3958
3959/*
3960 * If it's EXPLAIN ANALYZE, show instrumentation information for a plan node
3961 *
3962 * "which" identifies which instrumentation counter to print
3963 */
3964static void
3965show_instrumentation_count(const char *qlabel, int which,
3966						   PlanState *planstate, ExplainState *es)
3967{
3968	double		nfiltered;
3969	double		nloops;
3970
3971	if (!es->analyze || !planstate->instrument)
3972		return;
3973
3974	if (which == 2)
3975		nfiltered = planstate->instrument->nfiltered2;
3976	else
3977		nfiltered = planstate->instrument->nfiltered1;
3978	nloops = planstate->instrument->nloops;
3979
3980	/* In text mode, suppress zero counts; they're not interesting enough */
3981	if (nfiltered > 0 || es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
3982	{
3983		if (nloops > 0)
3984			ExplainPropertyFloat(qlabel, NULL, nfiltered / nloops, 0, es);
3985		else
3986			ExplainPropertyFloat(qlabel, NULL, 0.0, 0, es);
3987	}
3988}
3989
3990/*
3991 * Show extra information for a ForeignScan node.
3992 */
3993static void
3994show_foreignscan_info(ForeignScanState *fsstate, ExplainState *es)
3995{
3996	FdwRoutine *fdwroutine = fsstate->fdwroutine;
3997
3998	/* Let the FDW emit whatever fields it wants */
3999	if (((ForeignScan *) fsstate->ss.ps.plan)->operation != CMD_SELECT)
4000	{
4001		if (fdwroutine->ExplainDirectModify != NULL)
4002			fdwroutine->ExplainDirectModify(fsstate, es);
4003	}
4004	else
4005	{
4006		if (fdwroutine->ExplainForeignScan != NULL)
4007			fdwroutine->ExplainForeignScan(fsstate, es);
4008	}
4009}
4010
4011/*
4012 * Fetch the name of an index in an EXPLAIN
4013 *
4014 * We allow plugins to get control here so that plans involving hypothetical
4015 * indexes can be explained.
4016 *
4017 * Note: names returned by this function should be "raw"; the caller will
4018 * apply quoting if needed.  Formerly the convention was to do quoting here,
4019 * but we don't want that in non-text output formats.
4020 */
4021static const char *
4022explain_get_index_name(Oid indexId)
4023{
4024	const char *result;
4025
4026	if (explain_get_index_name_hook)
4027		result = (*explain_get_index_name_hook) (indexId);
4028	else
4029		result = NULL;
4030	if (result == NULL)
4031	{
4032		/* default behavior: look it up in the catalogs */
4033		result = get_rel_name(indexId);
4034		if (result == NULL)
4035			elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for index %u", indexId);
4036	}
4037	return result;
4038}
4039
4040/*
4041 * Return whether show_buffer_usage would have anything to print, if given
4042 * the same 'usage' data.  Note that when the format is anything other than
4043 * text, we print even if the counters are all zeroes.
4044 */
4045static bool
4046peek_buffer_usage(ExplainState *es, const BufferUsage *usage)
4047{
4048	bool		has_shared;
4049	bool		has_local;
4050	bool		has_temp;
4051	bool		has_shared_timing;
4052	bool		has_local_timing;
4053	bool		has_temp_timing;
4054
4055	if (usage == NULL)
4056		return false;
4057
4058	if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4059		return true;
4060
4061	has_shared = (usage->shared_blks_hit > 0 ||
4062				  usage->shared_blks_read > 0 ||
4063				  usage->shared_blks_dirtied > 0 ||
4064				  usage->shared_blks_written > 0);
4065	has_local = (usage->local_blks_hit > 0 ||
4066				 usage->local_blks_read > 0 ||
4067				 usage->local_blks_dirtied > 0 ||
4068				 usage->local_blks_written > 0);
4069	has_temp = (usage->temp_blks_read > 0 ||
4070				usage->temp_blks_written > 0);
4071	has_shared_timing = (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->shared_blk_read_time) ||
4072						 !INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->shared_blk_write_time));
4073	has_local_timing = (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->local_blk_read_time) ||
4074						!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->local_blk_write_time));
4075	has_temp_timing = (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->temp_blk_read_time) ||
4076					   !INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->temp_blk_write_time));
4077
4078	return has_shared || has_local || has_temp || has_shared_timing ||
4079		has_local_timing || has_temp_timing;
4080}
4081
4082/*
4083 * Show buffer usage details.  This better be sync with peek_buffer_usage.
4084 */
4085static void
4086show_buffer_usage(ExplainState *es, const BufferUsage *usage)
4087{
4088	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4089	{
4090		bool		has_shared = (usage->shared_blks_hit > 0 ||
4091								  usage->shared_blks_read > 0 ||
4092								  usage->shared_blks_dirtied > 0 ||
4093								  usage->shared_blks_written > 0);
4094		bool		has_local = (usage->local_blks_hit > 0 ||
4095								 usage->local_blks_read > 0 ||
4096								 usage->local_blks_dirtied > 0 ||
4097								 usage->local_blks_written > 0);
4098		bool		has_temp = (usage->temp_blks_read > 0 ||
4099								usage->temp_blks_written > 0);
4100		bool		has_shared_timing = (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->shared_blk_read_time) ||
4101										 !INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->shared_blk_write_time));
4102		bool		has_local_timing = (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->local_blk_read_time) ||
4103										!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->local_blk_write_time));
4104		bool		has_temp_timing = (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->temp_blk_read_time) ||
4105									   !INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->temp_blk_write_time));
4106
4107		/* Show only positive counter values. */
4108		if (has_shared || has_local || has_temp)
4109		{
4110			ExplainIndentText(es);
4111			appendStringInfoString(es->str, "Buffers:");
4112
4113			if (has_shared)
4114			{
4115				appendStringInfoString(es->str, " shared");
4116				if (usage->shared_blks_hit > 0)
4117					appendStringInfo(es->str, " hit=%" PRId64,
4118									 usage->shared_blks_hit);
4119				if (usage->shared_blks_read > 0)
4120					appendStringInfo(es->str, " read=%" PRId64,
4121									 usage->shared_blks_read);
4122				if (usage->shared_blks_dirtied > 0)
4123					appendStringInfo(es->str, " dirtied=%" PRId64,
4124									 usage->shared_blks_dirtied);
4125				if (usage->shared_blks_written > 0)
4126					appendStringInfo(es->str, " written=%" PRId64,
4127									 usage->shared_blks_written);
4128				if (has_local || has_temp)
4129					appendStringInfoChar(es->str, ',');
4130			}
4131			if (has_local)
4132			{
4133				appendStringInfoString(es->str, " local");
4134				if (usage->local_blks_hit > 0)
4135					appendStringInfo(es->str, " hit=%" PRId64,
4136									 usage->local_blks_hit);
4137				if (usage->local_blks_read > 0)
4138					appendStringInfo(es->str, " read=%" PRId64,
4139									 usage->local_blks_read);
4140				if (usage->local_blks_dirtied > 0)
4141					appendStringInfo(es->str, " dirtied=%" PRId64,
4142									 usage->local_blks_dirtied);
4143				if (usage->local_blks_written > 0)
4144					appendStringInfo(es->str, " written=%" PRId64,
4145									 usage->local_blks_written);
4146				if (has_temp)
4147					appendStringInfoChar(es->str, ',');
4148			}
4149			if (has_temp)
4150			{
4151				appendStringInfoString(es->str, " temp");
4152				if (usage->temp_blks_read > 0)
4153					appendStringInfo(es->str, " read=%" PRId64,
4154									 usage->temp_blks_read);
4155				if (usage->temp_blks_written > 0)
4156					appendStringInfo(es->str, " written=%" PRId64,
4157									 usage->temp_blks_written);
4158			}
4159			appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
4160		}
4161
4162		/* As above, show only positive counter values. */
4163		if (has_shared_timing || has_local_timing || has_temp_timing)
4164		{
4165			ExplainIndentText(es);
4166			appendStringInfoString(es->str, "I/O Timings:");
4167
4168			if (has_shared_timing)
4169			{
4170				appendStringInfoString(es->str, " shared");
4171				if (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->shared_blk_read_time))
4172					appendStringInfo(es->str, " read=%0.3f",
4173									 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->shared_blk_read_time));
4174				if (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->shared_blk_write_time))
4175					appendStringInfo(es->str, " write=%0.3f",
4176									 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->shared_blk_write_time));
4177				if (has_local_timing || has_temp_timing)
4178					appendStringInfoChar(es->str, ',');
4179			}
4180			if (has_local_timing)
4181			{
4182				appendStringInfoString(es->str, " local");
4183				if (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->local_blk_read_time))
4184					appendStringInfo(es->str, " read=%0.3f",
4185									 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->local_blk_read_time));
4186				if (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->local_blk_write_time))
4187					appendStringInfo(es->str, " write=%0.3f",
4188									 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->local_blk_write_time));
4189				if (has_temp_timing)
4190					appendStringInfoChar(es->str, ',');
4191			}
4192			if (has_temp_timing)
4193			{
4194				appendStringInfoString(es->str, " temp");
4195				if (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->temp_blk_read_time))
4196					appendStringInfo(es->str, " read=%0.3f",
4197									 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->temp_blk_read_time));
4198				if (!INSTR_TIME_IS_ZERO(usage->temp_blk_write_time))
4199					appendStringInfo(es->str, " write=%0.3f",
4200									 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->temp_blk_write_time));
4201			}
4202			appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
4203		}
4204	}
4205	else
4206	{
4207		ExplainPropertyInteger("Shared Hit Blocks", NULL,
4208							   usage->shared_blks_hit, es);
4209		ExplainPropertyInteger("Shared Read Blocks", NULL,
4210							   usage->shared_blks_read, es);
4211		ExplainPropertyInteger("Shared Dirtied Blocks", NULL,
4212							   usage->shared_blks_dirtied, es);
4213		ExplainPropertyInteger("Shared Written Blocks", NULL,
4214							   usage->shared_blks_written, es);
4215		ExplainPropertyInteger("Local Hit Blocks", NULL,
4216							   usage->local_blks_hit, es);
4217		ExplainPropertyInteger("Local Read Blocks", NULL,
4218							   usage->local_blks_read, es);
4219		ExplainPropertyInteger("Local Dirtied Blocks", NULL,
4220							   usage->local_blks_dirtied, es);
4221		ExplainPropertyInteger("Local Written Blocks", NULL,
4222							   usage->local_blks_written, es);
4223		ExplainPropertyInteger("Temp Read Blocks", NULL,
4224							   usage->temp_blks_read, es);
4225		ExplainPropertyInteger("Temp Written Blocks", NULL,
4226							   usage->temp_blks_written, es);
4227		if (track_io_timing)
4228		{
4229			ExplainPropertyFloat("Shared I/O Read Time", "ms",
4230								 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->shared_blk_read_time),
4231								 3, es);
4232			ExplainPropertyFloat("Shared I/O Write Time", "ms",
4233								 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->shared_blk_write_time),
4234								 3, es);
4235			ExplainPropertyFloat("Local I/O Read Time", "ms",
4236								 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->local_blk_read_time),
4237								 3, es);
4238			ExplainPropertyFloat("Local I/O Write Time", "ms",
4239								 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->local_blk_write_time),
4240								 3, es);
4241			ExplainPropertyFloat("Temp I/O Read Time", "ms",
4242								 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->temp_blk_read_time),
4243								 3, es);
4244			ExplainPropertyFloat("Temp I/O Write Time", "ms",
4245								 INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(usage->temp_blk_write_time),
4246								 3, es);
4247		}
4248	}
4249}
4250
4251/*
4252 * Show WAL usage details.
4253 */
4254static void
4255show_wal_usage(ExplainState *es, const WalUsage *usage)
4256{
4257	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4258	{
4259		/* Show only positive counter values. */
4260		if ((usage->wal_records > 0) || (usage->wal_fpi > 0) ||
4261			(usage->wal_bytes > 0) || (usage->wal_buffers_full > 0))
4262		{
4263			ExplainIndentText(es);
4264			appendStringInfoString(es->str, "WAL:");
4265
4266			if (usage->wal_records > 0)
4267				appendStringInfo(es->str, " records=%" PRId64,
4268								 usage->wal_records);
4269			if (usage->wal_fpi > 0)
4270				appendStringInfo(es->str, " fpi=%" PRId64,
4271								 usage->wal_fpi);
4272			if (usage->wal_bytes > 0)
4273				appendStringInfo(es->str, " bytes=%" PRIu64,
4274								 usage->wal_bytes);
4275			if (usage->wal_buffers_full > 0)
4276				appendStringInfo(es->str, " buffers full=%" PRId64,
4277								 usage->wal_buffers_full);
4278			appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
4279		}
4280	}
4281	else
4282	{
4283		ExplainPropertyInteger("WAL Records", NULL,
4284							   usage->wal_records, es);
4285		ExplainPropertyInteger("WAL FPI", NULL,
4286							   usage->wal_fpi, es);
4287		ExplainPropertyUInteger("WAL Bytes", NULL,
4288								usage->wal_bytes, es);
4289		ExplainPropertyInteger("WAL Buffers Full", NULL,
4290							   usage->wal_buffers_full, es);
4291	}
4292}
4293
4294/*
4295 * Show memory usage details.
4296 */
4297static void
4298show_memory_counters(ExplainState *es, const MemoryContextCounters *mem_counters)
4299{
4300	int64		memUsedkB = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(mem_counters->totalspace -
4301											   mem_counters->freespace);
4302	int64		memAllocatedkB = BYTES_TO_KILOBYTES(mem_counters->totalspace);
4303
4304	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4305	{
4306		ExplainIndentText(es);
4307		appendStringInfo(es->str,
4308						 "Memory: used=" INT64_FORMAT "kB  allocated=" INT64_FORMAT "kB",
4309						 memUsedkB, memAllocatedkB);
4310		appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
4311	}
4312	else
4313	{
4314		ExplainPropertyInteger("Memory Used", "kB", memUsedkB, es);
4315		ExplainPropertyInteger("Memory Allocated", "kB", memAllocatedkB, es);
4316	}
4317}
4318
4319
4320/*
4321 * Add some additional details about an IndexScan or IndexOnlyScan
4322 */
4323static void
4324ExplainIndexScanDetails(Oid indexid, ScanDirection indexorderdir,
4325						ExplainState *es)
4326{
4327	const char *indexname = explain_get_index_name(indexid);
4328
4329	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4330	{
4331		if (ScanDirectionIsBackward(indexorderdir))
4332			appendStringInfoString(es->str, " Backward");
4333		appendStringInfo(es->str, " using %s", quote_identifier(indexname));
4334	}
4335	else
4336	{
4337		const char *scandir;
4338
4339		switch (indexorderdir)
4340		{
4341			case BackwardScanDirection:
4342				scandir = "Backward";
4343				break;
4344			case ForwardScanDirection:
4345				scandir = "Forward";
4346				break;
4347			default:
4348				scandir = "???";
4349				break;
4350		}
4351		ExplainPropertyText("Scan Direction", scandir, es);
4352		ExplainPropertyText("Index Name", indexname, es);
4353	}
4354}
4355
4356/*
4357 * Show the target of a Scan node
4358 */
4359static void
4360ExplainScanTarget(Scan *plan, ExplainState *es)
4361{
4362	ExplainTargetRel((Plan *) plan, plan->scanrelid, es);
4363}
4364
4365/*
4366 * Show the target of a ModifyTable node
4367 *
4368 * Here we show the nominal target (ie, the relation that was named in the
4369 * original query).  If the actual target(s) is/are different, we'll show them
4370 * in show_modifytable_info().
4371 */
4372static void
4373ExplainModifyTarget(ModifyTable *plan, ExplainState *es)
4374{
4375	ExplainTargetRel((Plan *) plan, plan->nominalRelation, es);
4376}
4377
4378/*
4379 * Show the target relation of a scan or modify node
4380 */
4381static void
4382ExplainTargetRel(Plan *plan, Index rti, ExplainState *es)
4383{
4384	char	   *objectname = NULL;
4385	char	   *namespace = NULL;
4386	const char *objecttag = NULL;
4387	RangeTblEntry *rte;
4388	char	   *refname;
4389
4390	rte = rt_fetch(rti, es->rtable);
4391	refname = (char *) list_nth(es->rtable_names, rti - 1);
4392	if (refname == NULL)
4393		refname = rte->eref->aliasname;
4394
4395	switch (nodeTag(plan))
4396	{
4397		case T_SeqScan:
4398		case T_SampleScan:
4399		case T_IndexScan:
4400		case T_IndexOnlyScan:
4401		case T_BitmapHeapScan:
4402		case T_TidScan:
4403		case T_TidRangeScan:
4404		case T_ForeignScan:
4405		case T_CustomScan:
4406		case T_ModifyTable:
4407			/* Assert it's on a real relation */
4408			Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION);
4409			objectname = get_rel_name(rte->relid);
4410			if (es->verbose)
4411				namespace = get_namespace_name_or_temp(get_rel_namespace(rte->relid));
4412			objecttag = "Relation Name";
4413			break;
4414		case T_FunctionScan:
4415			{
4416				FunctionScan *fscan = (FunctionScan *) plan;
4417
4418				/* Assert it's on a RangeFunction */
4419				Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_FUNCTION);
4420
4421				/*
4422				 * If the expression is still a function call of a single
4423				 * function, we can get the real name of the function.
4424				 * Otherwise, punt.  (Even if it was a single function call
4425				 * originally, the optimizer could have simplified it away.)
4426				 */
4427				if (list_length(fscan->functions) == 1)
4428				{
4429					RangeTblFunction *rtfunc = (RangeTblFunction *) linitial(fscan->functions);
4430
4431					if (IsA(rtfunc->funcexpr, FuncExpr))
4432					{
4433						FuncExpr   *funcexpr = (FuncExpr *) rtfunc->funcexpr;
4434						Oid			funcid = funcexpr->funcid;
4435
4436						objectname = get_func_name(funcid);
4437						if (es->verbose)
4438							namespace = get_namespace_name_or_temp(get_func_namespace(funcid));
4439					}
4440				}
4441				objecttag = "Function Name";
4442			}
4443			break;
4444		case T_TableFuncScan:
4445			{
4446				TableFunc  *tablefunc = ((TableFuncScan *) plan)->tablefunc;
4447
4448				Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_TABLEFUNC);
4449				switch (tablefunc->functype)
4450				{
4451					case TFT_XMLTABLE:
4452						objectname = "xmltable";
4453						break;
4454					case TFT_JSON_TABLE:
4455						objectname = "json_table";
4456						break;
4457					default:
4458						elog(ERROR, "invalid TableFunc type %d",
4459							 (int) tablefunc->functype);
4460				}
4461				objecttag = "Table Function Name";
4462			}
4463			break;
4464		case T_ValuesScan:
4465			Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_VALUES);
4466			break;
4467		case T_CteScan:
4468			/* Assert it's on a non-self-reference CTE */
4469			Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_CTE);
4470			Assert(!rte->self_reference);
4471			objectname = rte->ctename;
4472			objecttag = "CTE Name";
4473			break;
4474		case T_NamedTuplestoreScan:
4475			Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_NAMEDTUPLESTORE);
4476			objectname = rte->enrname;
4477			objecttag = "Tuplestore Name";
4478			break;
4479		case T_WorkTableScan:
4480			/* Assert it's on a self-reference CTE */
4481			Assert(rte->rtekind == RTE_CTE);
4482			Assert(rte->self_reference);
4483			objectname = rte->ctename;
4484			objecttag = "CTE Name";
4485			break;
4486		default:
4487			break;
4488	}
4489
4490	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4491	{
4492		appendStringInfoString(es->str, " on");
4493		if (namespace != NULL)
4494			appendStringInfo(es->str, " %s.%s", quote_identifier(namespace),
4495							 quote_identifier(objectname));
4496		else if (objectname != NULL)
4497			appendStringInfo(es->str, " %s", quote_identifier(objectname));
4498		if (objectname == NULL || strcmp(refname, objectname) != 0)
4499			appendStringInfo(es->str, " %s", quote_identifier(refname));
4500	}
4501	else
4502	{
4503		if (objecttag != NULL && objectname != NULL)
4504			ExplainPropertyText(objecttag, objectname, es);
4505		if (namespace != NULL)
4506			ExplainPropertyText("Schema", namespace, es);
4507		ExplainPropertyText("Alias", refname, es);
4508	}
4509}
4510
4511/*
4512 * Show extra information for a ModifyTable node
4513 *
4514 * We have three objectives here.  First, if there's more than one target
4515 * table or it's different from the nominal target, identify the actual
4516 * target(s).  Second, give FDWs a chance to display extra info about foreign
4517 * targets.  Third, show information about ON CONFLICT.
4518 */
4519static void
4520show_modifytable_info(ModifyTableState *mtstate, List *ancestors,
4521					  ExplainState *es)
4522{
4523	ModifyTable *node = (ModifyTable *) mtstate->ps.plan;
4524	const char *operation;
4525	const char *foperation;
4526	bool		labeltargets;
4527	int			j;
4528	List	   *idxNames = NIL;
4529	ListCell   *lst;
4530
4531	switch (node->operation)
4532	{
4533		case CMD_INSERT:
4534			operation = "Insert";
4535			foperation = "Foreign Insert";
4536			break;
4537		case CMD_UPDATE:
4538			operation = "Update";
4539			foperation = "Foreign Update";
4540			break;
4541		case CMD_DELETE:
4542			operation = "Delete";
4543			foperation = "Foreign Delete";
4544			break;
4545		case CMD_MERGE:
4546			operation = "Merge";
4547			/* XXX unsupported for now, but avoid compiler noise */
4548			foperation = "Foreign Merge";
4549			break;
4550		default:
4551			operation = "???";
4552			foperation = "Foreign ???";
4553			break;
4554	}
4555
4556	/*
4557	 * Should we explicitly label target relations?
4558	 *
4559	 * If there's only one target relation, do not list it if it's the
4560	 * relation named in the query, or if it has been pruned.  (Normally
4561	 * mtstate->resultRelInfo doesn't include pruned relations, but a single
4562	 * pruned target relation may be present, if all other target relations
4563	 * have been pruned.  See ExecInitModifyTable().)
4564	 */
4565	labeltargets = (mtstate->mt_nrels > 1 ||
4566					(mtstate->mt_nrels == 1 &&
4567					 mtstate->resultRelInfo[0].ri_RangeTableIndex != node->nominalRelation &&
4568					 bms_is_member(mtstate->resultRelInfo[0].ri_RangeTableIndex,
4569								   mtstate->ps.state->es_unpruned_relids)));
4570
4571	if (labeltargets)
4572		ExplainOpenGroup("Target Tables", "Target Tables", false, es);
4573
4574	for (j = 0; j < mtstate->mt_nrels; j++)
4575	{
4576		ResultRelInfo *resultRelInfo = mtstate->resultRelInfo + j;
4577		FdwRoutine *fdwroutine = resultRelInfo->ri_FdwRoutine;
4578
4579		if (labeltargets)
4580		{
4581			/* Open a group for this target */
4582			ExplainOpenGroup("Target Table", NULL, true, es);
4583
4584			/*
4585			 * In text mode, decorate each target with operation type, so that
4586			 * ExplainTargetRel's output of " on foo" will read nicely.
4587			 */
4588			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4589			{
4590				ExplainIndentText(es);
4591				appendStringInfoString(es->str,
4592									   fdwroutine ? foperation : operation);
4593			}
4594
4595			/* Identify target */
4596			ExplainTargetRel((Plan *) node,
4597							 resultRelInfo->ri_RangeTableIndex,
4598							 es);
4599
4600			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4601			{
4602				appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
4603				es->indent++;
4604			}
4605		}
4606
4607		/* Give FDW a chance if needed */
4608		if (!resultRelInfo->ri_usesFdwDirectModify &&
4609			fdwroutine != NULL &&
4610			fdwroutine->ExplainForeignModify != NULL)
4611		{
4612			List	   *fdw_private = (List *) list_nth(node->fdwPrivLists, j);
4613
4614			fdwroutine->ExplainForeignModify(mtstate,
4615											 resultRelInfo,
4616											 fdw_private,
4617											 j,
4618											 es);
4619		}
4620
4621		if (labeltargets)
4622		{
4623			/* Undo the indentation we added in text format */
4624			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4625				es->indent--;
4626
4627			/* Close the group */
4628			ExplainCloseGroup("Target Table", NULL, true, es);
4629		}
4630	}
4631
4632	/* Gather names of ON CONFLICT arbiter indexes */
4633	foreach(lst, node->arbiterIndexes)
4634	{
4635		char	   *indexname = get_rel_name(lfirst_oid(lst));
4636
4637		idxNames = lappend(idxNames, indexname);
4638	}
4639
4640	if (node->onConflictAction != ONCONFLICT_NONE)
4641	{
4642		ExplainPropertyText("Conflict Resolution",
4643							node->onConflictAction == ONCONFLICT_NOTHING ?
4644							"NOTHING" : "UPDATE",
4645							es);
4646
4647		/*
4648		 * Don't display arbiter indexes at all when DO NOTHING variant
4649		 * implicitly ignores all conflicts
4650		 */
4651		if (idxNames)
4652			ExplainPropertyList("Conflict Arbiter Indexes", idxNames, es);
4653
4654		/* ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE qual is specially displayed */
4655		if (node->onConflictWhere)
4656		{
4657			show_upper_qual((List *) node->onConflictWhere, "Conflict Filter",
4658							&mtstate->ps, ancestors, es);
4659			show_instrumentation_count("Rows Removed by Conflict Filter", 1, &mtstate->ps, es);
4660		}
4661
4662		/* EXPLAIN ANALYZE display of actual outcome for each tuple proposed */
4663		if (es->analyze && mtstate->ps.instrument)
4664		{
4665			double		total;
4666			double		insert_path;
4667			double		other_path;
4668
4669			InstrEndLoop(outerPlanState(mtstate)->instrument);
4670
4671			/* count the number of source rows */
4672			total = outerPlanState(mtstate)->instrument->ntuples;
4673			other_path = mtstate->ps.instrument->ntuples2;
4674			insert_path = total - other_path;
4675
4676			ExplainPropertyFloat("Tuples Inserted", NULL,
4677								 insert_path, 0, es);
4678			ExplainPropertyFloat("Conflicting Tuples", NULL,
4679								 other_path, 0, es);
4680		}
4681	}
4682	else if (node->operation == CMD_MERGE)
4683	{
4684		/* EXPLAIN ANALYZE display of tuples processed */
4685		if (es->analyze && mtstate->ps.instrument)
4686		{
4687			double		total;
4688			double		insert_path;
4689			double		update_path;
4690			double		delete_path;
4691			double		skipped_path;
4692
4693			InstrEndLoop(outerPlanState(mtstate)->instrument);
4694
4695			/* count the number of source rows */
4696			total = outerPlanState(mtstate)->instrument->ntuples;
4697			insert_path = mtstate->mt_merge_inserted;
4698			update_path = mtstate->mt_merge_updated;
4699			delete_path = mtstate->mt_merge_deleted;
4700			skipped_path = total - insert_path - update_path - delete_path;
4701			Assert(skipped_path >= 0);
4702
4703			if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4704			{
4705				if (total > 0)
4706				{
4707					ExplainIndentText(es);
4708					appendStringInfoString(es->str, "Tuples:");
4709					if (insert_path > 0)
4710						appendStringInfo(es->str, " inserted=%.0f", insert_path);
4711					if (update_path > 0)
4712						appendStringInfo(es->str, " updated=%.0f", update_path);
4713					if (delete_path > 0)
4714						appendStringInfo(es->str, " deleted=%.0f", delete_path);
4715					if (skipped_path > 0)
4716						appendStringInfo(es->str, " skipped=%.0f", skipped_path);
4717					appendStringInfoChar(es->str, '\n');
4718				}
4719			}
4720			else
4721			{
4722				ExplainPropertyFloat("Tuples Inserted", NULL, insert_path, 0, es);
4723				ExplainPropertyFloat("Tuples Updated", NULL, update_path, 0, es);
4724				ExplainPropertyFloat("Tuples Deleted", NULL, delete_path, 0, es);
4725				ExplainPropertyFloat("Tuples Skipped", NULL, skipped_path, 0, es);
4726			}
4727		}
4728	}
4729
4730	if (labeltargets)
4731		ExplainCloseGroup("Target Tables", "Target Tables", false, es);
4732}
4733
4734/*
4735 * Explain the constituent plans of an Append, MergeAppend,
4736 * BitmapAnd, or BitmapOr node.
4737 *
4738 * The ancestors list should already contain the immediate parent of these
4739 * plans.
4740 */
4741static void
4742ExplainMemberNodes(PlanState **planstates, int nplans,
4743				   List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
4744{
4745	int			j;
4746
4747	for (j = 0; j < nplans; j++)
4748		ExplainNode(planstates[j], ancestors,
4749					"Member", NULL, es);
4750}
4751
4752/*
4753 * Report about any pruned subnodes of an Append or MergeAppend node.
4754 *
4755 * nplans indicates the number of live subplans.
4756 * nchildren indicates the original number of subnodes in the Plan;
4757 * some of these may have been pruned by the run-time pruning code.
4758 */
4759static void
4760ExplainMissingMembers(int nplans, int nchildren, ExplainState *es)
4761{
4762	if (nplans < nchildren || es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4763		ExplainPropertyInteger("Subplans Removed", NULL,
4764							   nchildren - nplans, es);
4765}
4766
4767/*
4768 * Explain a list of SubPlans (or initPlans, which also use SubPlan nodes).
4769 *
4770 * The ancestors list should already contain the immediate parent of these
4771 * SubPlans.
4772 */
4773static void
4774ExplainSubPlans(List *plans, List *ancestors,
4775				const char *relationship, ExplainState *es)
4776{
4777	ListCell   *lst;
4778
4779	foreach(lst, plans)
4780	{
4781		SubPlanState *sps = (SubPlanState *) lfirst(lst);
4782		SubPlan    *sp = sps->subplan;
4783
4784		/*
4785		 * There can be multiple SubPlan nodes referencing the same physical
4786		 * subplan (same plan_id, which is its index in PlannedStmt.subplans).
4787		 * We should print a subplan only once, so track which ones we already
4788		 * printed.  This state must be global across the plan tree, since the
4789		 * duplicate nodes could be in different plan nodes, eg both a bitmap
4790		 * indexscan's indexqual and its parent heapscan's recheck qual.  (We
4791		 * do not worry too much about which plan node we show the subplan as
4792		 * attached to in such cases.)
4793		 */
4794		if (bms_is_member(sp->plan_id, es->printed_subplans))
4795			continue;
4796		es->printed_subplans = bms_add_member(es->printed_subplans,
4797											  sp->plan_id);
4798
4799		/*
4800		 * Treat the SubPlan node as an ancestor of the plan node(s) within
4801		 * it, so that ruleutils.c can find the referents of subplan
4802		 * parameters.
4803		 */
4804		ancestors = lcons(sp, ancestors);
4805
4806		ExplainNode(sps->planstate, ancestors,
4807					relationship, sp->plan_name, es);
4808
4809		ancestors = list_delete_first(ancestors);
4810	}
4811}
4812
4813/*
4814 * Explain a list of children of a CustomScan.
4815 */
4816static void
4817ExplainCustomChildren(CustomScanState *css, List *ancestors, ExplainState *es)
4818{
4819	ListCell   *cell;
4820	const char *label =
4821		(list_length(css->custom_ps) != 1 ? "children" : "child");
4822
4823	foreach(cell, css->custom_ps)
4824		ExplainNode((PlanState *) lfirst(cell), ancestors, label, NULL, es);
4825}
4826
4827/*
4828 * Create a per-plan-node workspace for collecting per-worker data.
4829 *
4830 * Output related to each worker will be temporarily "set aside" into a
4831 * separate buffer, which we'll merge into the main output stream once
4832 * we've processed all data for the plan node.  This makes it feasible to
4833 * generate a coherent sub-group of fields for each worker, even though the
4834 * code that produces the fields is in several different places in this file.
4835 * Formatting of such a set-aside field group is managed by
4836 * ExplainOpenSetAsideGroup and ExplainSaveGroup/ExplainRestoreGroup.
4837 */
4838static ExplainWorkersState *
4839ExplainCreateWorkersState(int num_workers)
4840{
4841	ExplainWorkersState *wstate;
4842
4843	wstate = (ExplainWorkersState *) palloc(sizeof(ExplainWorkersState));
4844	wstate->num_workers = num_workers;
4845	wstate->worker_inited = (bool *) palloc0(num_workers * sizeof(bool));
4846	wstate->worker_str = (StringInfoData *)
4847		palloc0(num_workers * sizeof(StringInfoData));
4848	wstate->worker_state_save = (int *) palloc(num_workers * sizeof(int));
4849	return wstate;
4850}
4851
4852/*
4853 * Begin or resume output into the set-aside group for worker N.
4854 */
4855static void
4856ExplainOpenWorker(int n, ExplainState *es)
4857{
4858	ExplainWorkersState *wstate = es->workers_state;
4859
4860	Assert(wstate);
4861	Assert(n >= 0 && n < wstate->num_workers);
4862
4863	/* Save prior output buffer pointer */
4864	wstate->prev_str = es->str;
4865
4866	if (!wstate->worker_inited[n])
4867	{
4868		/* First time through, so create the buffer for this worker */
4869		initStringInfo(&wstate->worker_str[n]);
4870		es->str = &wstate->worker_str[n];
4871
4872		/*
4873		 * Push suitable initial formatting state for this worker's field
4874		 * group.  We allow one extra logical nesting level, since this group
4875		 * will eventually be wrapped in an outer "Workers" group.
4876		 */
4877		ExplainOpenSetAsideGroup("Worker", NULL, true, 2, es);
4878
4879		/*
4880		 * In non-TEXT formats we always emit a "Worker Number" field, even if
4881		 * there's no other data for this worker.
4882		 */
4883		if (es->format != EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4884			ExplainPropertyInteger("Worker Number", NULL, n, es);
4885
4886		wstate->worker_inited[n] = true;
4887	}
4888	else
4889	{
4890		/* Resuming output for a worker we've already emitted some data for */
4891		es->str = &wstate->worker_str[n];
4892
4893		/* Restore formatting state saved by last ExplainCloseWorker() */
4894		ExplainRestoreGroup(es, 2, &wstate->worker_state_save[n]);
4895	}
4896
4897	/*
4898	 * In TEXT format, prefix the first output line for this worker with
4899	 * "Worker N:".  Then, any additional lines should be indented one more
4900	 * stop than the "Worker N" line is.
4901	 */
4902	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4903	{
4904		if (es->str->len == 0)
4905		{
4906			ExplainIndentText(es);
4907			appendStringInfo(es->str, "Worker %d:  ", n);
4908		}
4909
4910		es->indent++;
4911	}
4912}
4913
4914/*
4915 * End output for worker N --- must pair with previous ExplainOpenWorker call
4916 */
4917static void
4918ExplainCloseWorker(int n, ExplainState *es)
4919{
4920	ExplainWorkersState *wstate = es->workers_state;
4921
4922	Assert(wstate);
4923	Assert(n >= 0 && n < wstate->num_workers);
4924	Assert(wstate->worker_inited[n]);
4925
4926	/*
4927	 * Save formatting state in case we do another ExplainOpenWorker(), then
4928	 * pop the formatting stack.
4929	 */
4930	ExplainSaveGroup(es, 2, &wstate->worker_state_save[n]);
4931
4932	/*
4933	 * In TEXT format, if we didn't actually produce any output line(s) then
4934	 * truncate off the partial line emitted by ExplainOpenWorker.  (This is
4935	 * to avoid bogus output if, say, show_buffer_usage chooses not to print
4936	 * anything for the worker.)  Also fix up the indent level.
4937	 */
4938	if (es->format == EXPLAIN_FORMAT_TEXT)
4939	{
4940		while (es->str->len > 0 && es->str->data[es->str->len - 1] != '\n')
4941			es->str->data[--(es->str->len)] = '\0';
4942
4943		es->indent--;
4944	}
4945
4946	/* Restore prior output buffer pointer */
4947	es->str = wstate->prev_str;
4948}
4949
4950/*
4951 * Print per-worker info for current node, then free the ExplainWorkersState.
4952 */
4953static void
4954ExplainFlushWorkersState(ExplainState *es)
4955{
4956	ExplainWorkersState *wstate = es->workers_state;
4957
4958	ExplainOpenGroup("Workers", "Workers", false, es);
4959	for (int i = 0; i < wstate->num_workers; i++)
4960	{
4961		if (wstate->worker_inited[i])
4962		{
4963			/* This must match previous ExplainOpenSetAsideGroup call */
4964			ExplainOpenGroup("Worker", NULL, true, es);
4965			appendStringInfoString(es->str, wstate->worker_str[i].data);
4966			ExplainCloseGroup("Worker", NULL, true, es);
4967
4968			pfree(wstate->worker_str[i].data);
4969		}
4970	}
4971	ExplainCloseGroup("Workers", "Workers", false, es);
4972
4973	pfree(wstate->worker_inited);
4974	pfree(wstate->worker_str);
4975	pfree(wstate->worker_state_save);
4976	pfree(wstate);
4977}
